reason: The remediation modifies the /etc/fstab entry for /dev/shm, a filesystem mount point. Changing mount options can cause services that rely on executing binaries from /dev/shm to fail, potentially leading to system instability or loss of functionality. According to policy, filesystem/partition changes are classified as too dangerous to remediate automatically.
estimated_impact: reboot required; filesystems affected; Potential disruption of applications that use /dev/shm for executable code; Requires system reboot or remount, which may affect uptime; May impact container runtimes or services that expect exec permission on shared memory
